checklist.item: SKILL.md has a numbered or bulleted 'Anti-Pattern' or 'Pitfall' section listing at least 5 distinct forbidden patterns with prohibitive language ('NO', 'NEVER', or 'forbidden')
SKILL.md explicitly bans 'badge' or 'pill' above headings (the word 'badge' appears in a prohibition context)
SKILL.md explicitly bans the centered 'badge/heading/subtext/CTA' stack pattern
SKILL.md explicitly bans 'floating decorative shapes' or 'blobs' or 'bob animation'

references/anti-patterns.md exists with at least 40 lines and each forbidden pattern has a concrete REPLACEMENT technique (not just 'don't do X' but 'instead do Y' with specific CSS or layout alternative)
prompt-template.md contains a CSS variable block with ALL of: --background, --foreground, --primary, --accent, --muted, --border (at least 6 of these variable names present)
prompt-template.md references Google Fonts import (the string 'fonts.googleapis.com' or 'Google Fonts' appears)
prompt-template.md includes Tailwind config extension with fontFamily keys (strings 'font-heading' AND 'font-body' both appear)
prompt-template.md includes responsive rules (at least 2 of: 'sm:', 'md:', 'lg:', 'xl:', 'responsive', 'breakpoint', 'mobile' appear)
prompt-template.md includes a dependencies or packages section (the word 'dependencies' or 'packages' appears as a heading or label)
prompt-template.md has a Typography section (heading containing 'Typography' or 'Font')
prompt-template.md has a Color system section (heading containing 'Color' or CSS variables with HSL values)
prompt-template.md has a Spacing section (word 'spacing' or 'padding' or 'gap' in a heading or labeled section)
prompt-template.md has an Effects or Animation section (heading containing 'Effect' or 'Animation')
prompt-template.md has Section layout specs (heading containing 'Section' with layout details)
prompt-template.md has Responsive rules (word 'Responsive' in a heading)
prompt-template.md mentions accessibility (word 'accessibility' or 'a11y' or 'aria' or 'contrast' appears)
prompt-template.md has a Dependencies section (heading containing 'Dependencies' or 'Packages')
prompt-template.md has a Key Patterns or Component patterns section (heading containing 'Pattern' or 'Component')
prompt-template.md mentions dark/light mode (strings 'dark' and 'light' or 'theme' appear in mode-switching context)
color-palettes.md uses HSL format for color variable definitions (numbers like '220 15% 8%' or 'hsl(220, 15%, 8%)') — no hex #RRGGBB or rgb() in CSS variable assignments
effects-catalog.md Tailwind classes are real v3/v4 utilities — spot-check 5 classes: recognizable standard classes (bg-*, text-*, rounded-*, shadow-*, blur-*, backdrop-*, etc.), not invented names
design-presets.md font names are real Google Fonts — spot-check 5 font names: well-known real fonts (Inter, Space Grotesk, JetBrains Mono, Instrument Serif, Barlow, etc.)
effects-catalog.md keyframe animations use valid CSS syntax: @keyframes with percentage stops or from/to, valid property names (transform, opacity, filter, etc.)
CSS custom properties across all 3 files use valid --variable-name: value syntax (double-dash prefix, no spaces in variable name)

D5.name: Conversation Efficiency
D5.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md conversation flow. Score 0-10 using STRICT checks:

(A) PHASE COUNT (0-3 pts): Count numbered phases. 5-6 phases = 3pts. 7-8 = 2pts. 9+ = 1pt. Under 5 = 0pts.

(B) CHECKPOINT EFFICIENCY (0-3 pts): Count 'CHECKPOINT' markers. Checkpoints should equal phase count (one per phase). Checkpoints = phases +/-1 = 3pts. 2+ fewer checkpoints than phases = 1pt. No checkpoints = 0pts.

(C) BATCH APPROACH (0-2 pts): Does Phase 5 (sections) offer a batch or grouped approach for 8-12 sections without requiring individual turns per section? Explicit batch = 2pts. Some grouping = 1pt. One-per-turn only = 0pts.

(D) FAST PATH (0-2 pts): Shortcut for experienced users who provide all info upfront? Explicit shortcut = 2pts. Implied = 1pt. None = 0pts.

Sum A+B+C+D. Max 10.

D8.name: Layout Creativity
D8.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/references/section-patterns.md completely. Count UNIQUE layout compositions by SPATIAL GEOMETRY — two layouts are different only if their spatial arrangement differs (e.g., 'asymmetric split' vs 'stacked overlap' vs 'orbital/radial' vs 'masonry cascade' vs 'full-bleed typography'). Column count variations ('3-col grid' vs '4-col grid') are ONE geometry.

Method: Find each named layout pattern. Note its spatial geometry in 3 words. Group by geometry. Count groups.

Score STRICTLY: 0-3 geometries = 2. 4-6 = 4. 7-9 = 6. 10-12 = 8. 13+ = 10.

checklist.item: inspiration-nature.md contains at least 5 named themes, each with a markdown ingredients table (3+ data rows) AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-urban.md contains at least 5 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-material.md contains at least 5 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-art.md contains at least 3 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-era.md contains at least 3 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-cinema.md contains at least 3 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
inspiration-mood.md contains at least 5 named themes, each with ingredients table AND color schema with HSL values
The FIRST theme in inspiration-nature.md has sensory anchors (words evoking sight, sound, touch, or smell — not just abstract color names)
The FIRST theme in inspiration-nature.md lists at least 6 composable CSS ingredients (distinct techniques: gradient, blur, texture, shadow, animation, filter, etc.)
The FIRST theme in inspiration-urban.md has sensory anchors AND at least 6 composable CSS ingredients
At least 3 themes across both files include mix-and-match notes or combinability guidance (how to combine with other categories)
Color schemas in both files use specific HSL numbers (e.g., '25 80% 55%') — not vague descriptions like 'warm tones'

D13.name: Tone/Mood Diversity
D13.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/references/inspiration-mood.md. List every named mood/tone/atmosphere theme. Group synonyms into emotional archetypes: (serene/calm/peaceful = 1), (energetic/dynamic/vibrant = 1), (mysterious/enigmatic = 1), (playful/whimsical/fun = 1), (dark/brooding/moody = 1), (luxurious/opulent = 1), (nostalgic/vintage = 1), (chaotic/raw/punk = 1), etc.

Count unique archetypes represented.

Score STRICTLY: 0-3 = 2. 4-6 = 4. 7-9 = 6. 10-12 = 8. 13+ = 10.

checklist.item: inspiration-guide.md contains a compatibility matrix or section (a table or structured list showing which theme categories pair well)
inspiration-guide.md has at least 5 concrete cross-category mixing examples (combining ingredients from 2+ different inspiration files with specific CSS/visual results)
inspiration-guide.md has an intensity dial or spectrum system (e.g., whisper/medium/bold or subtle/moderate/full-canvas) with application guidance
inspiration-guide.md has at least 10 named mix recipes or combination examples (each with recipe name and ingredient list from multiple categories)
inspiration-guide.md has a random tone generator or serendipity mechanism encouraging unexpected combinations
SKILL.md or inspiration-guide.md contains an anti-slop or anti-generic named section (words 'slop', 'generic', 'cliche', 'default trap', or 'unique identity' appear in a section heading or mandate)
The text explicitly calls out 'premium dark SaaS' or 'dark gradient landing page' as a lazy default to avoid (the phrase 'premium dark' or 'dark SaaS' appears in cautionary context)
SKILL.md Phase 1 drives through inspiration-based direction before committing — presents aesthetic bundles or tone galleries, not just 'pick light or dark'

SKILL.md uses exploratory/creative language in workflow phases (at least 3 of: 'explore', 'canvas', 'palette', 'discover', 'surprise', 'morph' appear — not just menu language like 'select', 'choose from list')

inspiration-guide.md or SKILL.md includes a mechanism for random or unexpected combinations pushing users beyond comfort zone (words 'random', 'surprise', 'wild card', or 'serendipity' in a feature description)
inspiration-nature.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables (markdown table with 3+ data rows per theme)
inspiration-urban.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables
inspiration-material.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables
inspiration-era.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables
inspiration-art.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables
inspiration-cinema.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables
inspiration-mood.md has 3+ themes with ingredient tables

D19.name: Guide vs Rule Ratio
D19.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/references/inspiration-guide.md. Count rigid words (R): 'must', 'always', 'never', 'required', 'shall'. Count guiding words (G): 'consider', 'explore', 'try', 'experiment', 'might', 'could', 'suggest', 'inspiration', 'starting point'.

Ratio = G / (R + G). If R = 0, ratio = 1.0.

Base score: Ratio 0.8+ = 8pts. 0.6-0.79 = 6pts. 0.4-0.59 = 4pts. Under 0.4 = 2pts.

Bonus +2pts if file contains explicit flexibility callout ('not a locked theme', 'starting point', 'feel free to adapt', or 'loose inspiration').

Cap at 10.

D20.name: Canvas Metaphor Fidelity
D20.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md. Score 0-10:

(1) EXPLORATION (0-2): Phase 1 presents aesthetic BUNDLES with evocative names (e.g., 'Midnight Glass', 'Neon Edge') not flat option lists? Named bundles with descriptions = 2pts. Flat lists = 0pts.

(2) SURPRISE (0-2): At least 3 aesthetically DIVERSE bundles offered simultaneously (dark+light+colorful, not 3 dark variations)? Diverse = 2pts. Similar = 0pts.

(3) SENSORY LANGUAGE (0-2): Count sensory/emotional adjectives in Phase 1 and Phase 4 descriptions ('warm', 'crisp', 'electric', 'liquid', 'organic'). 5+ = 2pts. 2-4 = 1pt. 0-1 = 0pts.

(4) MORPHING (0-2): Skill describes combining or morphing ideas from different themes (e.g., 'mix ingredients from nature + urban')? Present = 2pts. Absent = 0pts.

(5) ROLLBACK (0-2): User can revisit earlier choices after seeing later options? Explicit rollback mechanism = 2pts. Implied = 1pt. Absent = 0pts.

Sum. Max 10.

checklist.item: color-palettes.md exists and contains at least 12 distinctly named color tone palettes (count distinct heading-level names like 'Warm Earth', 'Cool Ocean', 'Jewel', 'Pastel', etc.)
At least 8 palettes have FULL CSS variable blocks with HSL values for: background, foreground, primary, accent, muted, border (all 6 variables present per palette)
SKILL.md Phase 1 or Phase 3 explicitly asks the user to choose a color TONE or MOOD (words 'tone', 'palette', or 'color mood' in a user-facing question)
At least 8 palettes in color-palettes.md include emotional or sensory descriptions (e.g., 'earthy warmth', 'ocean depth' — not just color names)
color-palettes.md provides intensity variants: words 'whisper' AND 'bold' (or 'light' AND 'saturated') appear as variant labels, with at least 5 tones having 2+ intensity variants
SKILL.md or anti-patterns.md explicitly bans the default two-column hero (phrase 'two-column' or 'text left image right' or 'side-by-side hero' appears in a prohibition)
section-patterns.md contains at least 5 NAMED hero layout alternatives that are genuinely unconventional (distinct names — not column-count variations)
SKILL.md or anti-patterns.md explicitly bans basic rectangular/rounded-square image containers (with alternatives like clip-path, organic shapes, or masked containers listed)
section-patterns.md uses specific spatial CSS properties (at least 3 of: overlap percentages, rotation degrees, z-index values, blend modes, negative margins appear across patterns)
anti-patterns.md contains explicit ban text for BOTH a generic hero pattern AND a generic image container pattern
SKILL.md Phase 3 states a single font family rule — text says to default to ONE typeface with weight contrast (words 'single family', 'one typeface', or 'weight contrast' appear)
typography-heuristics.md exists and specifies heading letter-spacing as negative: tracking-tight or tighter (-0.025em or -2% to -3% range mentioned)
typography-heuristics.md specifies heading line-height as 110-120% (values 'leading-[1.1]' or 'leading-[1.2]' or '110%' or '120%' appear)
typography-heuristics.md or SKILL.md states a maximum font size count (the number '6' or word 'six' appears in context of max font sizes or type scale limit)
design-presets.md includes at least 3 single-family font pairing entries where heading and body use the SAME font family with different weights (e.g., 'Inter 800' + 'Inter 400')

animation-storytelling.md exists with at least 100 lines and contains concept of 'animation narrative' or 'scroll journey' or 'narrative arc' (page tells a story through animation, not just per-element effects)
animation-storytelling.md contains at least 3 NAMED narrative arc templates (e.g., 'Emergence', 'Awakening', 'Descent') each with opening/midpoint/conclusion structure
At least 2 arcs describe animations cinematically with environmental context (words like 'light fills', 'environment builds', 'atmosphere shifts' — not just 'opacity 0 to 1')

animation-storytelling.md describes chaining 4+ simultaneous properties over scroll ranges ('useTransform' or 'scroll' alongside 4+ CSS properties: scale, blur, opacity, saturation, color in one sequence)
animation-storytelling.md includes audio atmosphere or sound suggestions tied to scroll (word 'audio' or 'sound' or 'ambient' in scroll-linked context)
SKILL.md contains explicit rule against 'hard divisions' or 'different background colors' between sections (words 'hard division', 'no hard', 'continuity', or 'flow into each other' in a rule)

section-patterns.md or SKILL.md describes at least 3 specific section transition techniques (e.g., shared gradient, overlapping content, continuous scroll animation, morphing backgrounds, gradient mask — 3+ distinct named techniques)
prompt-template.md contains a 'SECTION FLOW' or 'ANIMATION NARRATIVE' or 'SCROLL JOURNEY' labeled block in its output structure
section-patterns.md describes inter-section scroll animations involving transformations (not just fade — words 'morph', 'parallax', 'depth', 'color shift', or 'transform' in inter-section context)
SKILL.md or section-patterns.md includes at least 2 example narrative flows showing sections connecting as continuous experience (multi-step journey like 'void/environment/content/energy/resolution')
A 'Cartoon' or 'Illustrated' or 'Hand-drawn' domain exists in any references/ file with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Minimalism' or 'Monolithic' or 'Monochrome single-color' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Pixel art' or 'Retro 8-bit' or 'Retro gaming' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
An 'Outer Space' or 'Cosmic' or 'Nebula' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
An 'Underwater' or 'Ocean depth' or 'Aquatic' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Steampunk' or 'Mechanical' or 'Clockwork' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Paper' or 'Stationery' or 'Origami' or 'Ink' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Neon City' or 'Cyberpunk' or 'Hologram' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
A 'Clay' or 'Claymation' or '3D tactile' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
An 'Isometric' or 'Blueprint' or 'Technical drawing' domain exists with ingredients table + color schema
SKILL.md contains per-phase lazy loading with 'ONLY load' language — a table or per-phase directive specifying which SINGLE file to load at each phase, with 'do NOT load other references' prohibition
SKILL.md contains a 'Context Budget' or 'Context Engineering' warning section about context window consumption and total token load
SKILL.md contains a File Index table listing each reference file with approximate line count AND content summary (agent can decide relevance without reading)
At least 5 reference .md files start within first 5 lines with a brief summary comment (HTML comment or short paragraph) describing contents
Reference files are self-contained: spot-check 3 reference files — each understandable without reading other references (no mandatory 'see X.md for required context' dependencies)

D28.name: Instruction Density & Signal-to-Noise
D28.eval_prompt:
Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md completely. Score 0-9 using four independent checks:

(A) TOTAL LINE COUNT (0-3 pts): Count last line number. Under 200 = 3pts. 200-299 = 2pts. 300-399 = 1pt. 400+ = 0pts.

(B) REFERENCE DELEGATION (0-2 pts): Per-phase lazy-load table or 'load ONLY references/X.md' directives. 5+ phase-to-file mappings = 2pts. 1-4 or vague = 1pt. None = 0pts.

(C) ANTI-PATTERN SECTION CONCISENESS (0-2 pts): Find 'Pitfall'/'Anti-pattern' section, count lines to next ## heading. Under 25 lines = 2pts. 25-65 = 1pt. Over 65 = 0pts. No such section = 2pts.

(D) PHASE CONCISENESS (0-2 pts): Count lines per phase. Phases 1-5 limit: 35 lines each. Last phase (generation) limit: 50 lines. ALL meet limits = 2pts. Exactly 1 exceeds = 1pt. 2+ exceed = 0pts.

Sum A+B+C+D. Cross-context repetition is intentional — do NOT penalize the same rule appearing in multiple sections. Min 0, max 9.

D29.name: Vertical Slice Checkpoints
D29.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md conversation flow (Phases 1-6). Score 0-10: Does the skill enforce VERTICAL SLICES with human verification checkpoints between phases? STRICT scoring: (1) Each phase ends with explicit human confirmation before proceeding — not just 'Proceed to Phase N' but 'Wait for user to confirm/adjust before moving on'. All 6 phases have this = 2pts, most = 1pt, few/none = 0pts. (2) Phase 6 (generation) has intermediate checkpoints — does it generate the ENTIRE prompt in one shot, or does it build incrementally (e.g., generate design system first → confirm → generate sections → confirm)? Single-shot generation = 0pts, 2+ checkpoints in generation = 2pts. (3) Rollback mechanism — can the user say 'redo Phase 3' without losing Phase 1-2 work? Explicit = 2pts, implicit = 1pt, missing = 0pts. (4) Each phase produces a VISIBLE artifact the user can review (not just internal state) — 2pts if all phases show their output, 0pts if phases silently accumulate. (5) Error recovery — what happens if user gives contradictory inputs across phases? Addressed = 2pts, not addressed = 0pts.

D30.name: Reference Compression Ratio
D30.eval_prompt: Read these 3 specific files completely: {skill_path}/references/section-patterns.md, {skill_path}/references/effects-catalog.md, {skill_path}/references/design-presets.md. Score 0-10 on information DENSITY — penalize bloat: (1) Format: Are entries structured as scannable tables or compact lists (name | CSS values | usage context) rather than multi-paragraph prose descriptions? Read 5 entries from each file. Tables/lists = 2pts, mixed = 1pt, mostly paragraphs = 0pts. (2) Redundancy: In each file, search for repeated CSS patterns (e.g., same gradient, same shadow, same animation). Use Grep for common patterns like 'linear-gradient', 'box-shadow', 'backdrop-blur'. Any value appearing in 3+ separate entries = redundant. 0 redundancies = 2pts, 1-3 = 1pt, 4+ = 0pts. (3) Actionability: Sample 5 entries per file — does each provide CONCRETE CSS/Tailwind classes ready to copy, or abstract advice like 'use warm tones'? All concrete = 2pts, mixed = 1pt, mostly abstract = 0pts. (4) Entry compactness: Count lines per entry/theme/pattern in each file. Average over 20 lines per entry = 0pts, 10-20 = 1pt, under 10 = 2pts. (5) Self-contained: Can each entry be understood without reading surrounding entries? Spot-check 3 entries per file. All standalone = 2pts, some depend on context = 1pt, requires reading whole file = 0pts.

D31.name: Deterministic Research-Generation Separation
D31.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md. Score 0-10: Does the skill SEPARATE research/curation (reading references, browsing options) from generation (writing the final prompt)? This is critical for preventing hallucination. STRICT: (1) Phases 1-5 are clearly RESEARCH/CURATION phases — they gather, curate, and confirm choices. Phase 6 is GENERATION — it assembles the confirmed choices into output. Is this separation explicit? Clear = 2pts, implied = 1pt, blurred = 0pts. (2) Does the skill instruct the agent to NOT generate CSS/code during curation phases? (Prevents premature commitment to hallucinated values). Explicit prohibition = 2pts, silent = 0pts. (3) Generation phase (6) references ONLY confirmed user choices from prior phases — not re-reading references or making new creative decisions. Explicit constraint = 2pts, not mentioned = 0pts. (4) Are aesthetic suggestions in early phases presented as OPTIONS (multiple choices) rather than single recommendations? Forces exploration over premature lock-in. Multiple options required = 2pts, single suggestions = 0pts. (5) Does the skill warn against the agent 'filling in gaps' with its own opinions during generation? Gaps should trigger a follow-up question, not a guess. Warning present = 2pts, absent = 0pts.

D32.name: Multi-Agent Tree Generation Architecture
D32.eval_prompt: Read {skill_path}/SKILL.md, focusing on Phase 6 (generation) and the overall architecture. Score 0-10: Does the skill mandate MULTI-AGENT, MULTI-TURN generation instead of single-shot monolithic output? This is the #1 quality lever — a single agent generating 500+ lines of design prompt in one turn WILL produce slop. STRICT scoring: (1) Phase 6 explicitly instructs spawning SUB-AGENTS (via Agent tool or claude CLI) for different generation tasks — e.g., one agent for design system CSS, another for section layouts, another for animation narrative. Must use words like 'spawn', 'sub-agent', 'delegate', 'Agent tool', or 'parallel agents'. Explicit multi-agent = 3pts, single-agent hinted = 1pt, not mentioned = 0pts. (2) Generation is LAYERED/RECURSIVE — a coordinator agent assembles outputs from specialist agents, not one agent doing everything sequentially. Tree-expanding pattern described = 2pts, flat sequential = 0pts. (3) Each sub-agent gets MINIMAL context — only the confirmed choices relevant to its task + its specific reference file. Context scoping per agent = 2pts, all context to all agents = 0pts. (4) Sub-agent outputs are VALIDATED before assembly — coordinator checks for anti-patterns, consistency, conflicts between sub-agent outputs. Validation step = 2pts, blind assembly = 0pts. (5) Final assembly includes a REVIEW pass — after combining all sub-agent outputs, a fresh agent (or the coordinator) reviews the complete prompt for coherence and anti-pattern violations. Review pass = 1pt, no review = 0pts. Score 0 = single agent generates entire prompt in one turn. This criterion CANNOT score above 2 if multi-agent generation is not explicitly required.
